francophone.excerpt: Après 16 ans au pouvoir, Viktor Orbán, chantre de l’illibéralisme, a reconnu dimanche «une défaite douloureuse mais sans ambiguïté».

francophone.excerpt: Cette condamnation constitue un "signal fort en faveur de la transparence des pratiques commerciales dans le secteur des télécommunications", a salué l'association UFC-Que Choisir.

francophone.excerpt: Après plusieurs jours de manifestations, Dublin a annoncé de nouvelles baisses de taxes et un plan de soutien pour contenir la flambée des prix liée à la guerre au Moyen-Orient.

anglophone.excerpt: The team was returning from a match when they were attacked by "masked men wielding guns", officials say.

anglophone.excerpt: President Trump announced a blockade of Iranian ports after peace talks with Iran collapsed. And, Viktor Orbán concedes defeat after 16 years in power in Hungary.

anglophone.excerpt: The U.S. Central Command said the blockade would be "enforced impartially against vessels of all nations" from 10 a.m. EDT, following the breakdown of talks after 21 hours of negotiations.

anglophone.excerpt: On Sunday, President Trump said the U.S. would blockade the Strait of Hormuz after negotiations between the U.S. and Iran broke down over the weekend.

anglophone.title: Retired U.S. Navy admiral on Trump's threat to blockade the Strait of Hormuz
anglophone.excerpt: NPR's Michel Martin speaks to retired U.S. Navy Adm. James Foggo, dean of the Center for Maritime Strategy, about President Trump's command to blockade the Strait of Hormuz.

anglophone.title: Pope Leo stands firm with Trump as Vatican–Washington rift deepens over Iran war
anglophone.excerpt: Pope Leo XIV says he will not be deterred by criticism from President Trump, vowing to continue his calls for peace as tensions escalate between the Vatican and Washington over the Iran conflict.

anglophone.title: What a chimpanzee 'civil war' can teach us about how societies fall apart
anglophone.excerpt: A long-term study of the world's largest known community of chimpanzees has documented a rare event: what the researchers describe as the primate equivalent of a "civil war."

anglophone.title: The FAA takes a rare step to head off a traffic jam at Chicago's O'Hare Airport
anglophone.excerpt: Federal regulators want airlines to cut the number of flights at O'Hare Airport in Chicago this summer. It's an unusual move, sparked by a turf war between two major airlines with hubs at the airport.

anglophone.title: 2025 was one of most volatile years ever for U.S. naturalizations
anglophone.excerpt: The year began with many people becoming U.S. citizens, but by December, fewer people were doing so, driven by ramped-up scrutiny of applications and eroding trust in the system.

anglophone.title: How a $75 billion windfall from Congress has insulated ICE
anglophone.excerpt: Last year, Congress approved $75 billion for immigration enforcement. That money has allowed ICE to operate nearly unfettered during a record-long shutdown of the Department of Homeland Security.

anglophone.title: More than a quarter of private colleges are at risk of closing, a new projection shows
